attacking_style: Attacks from established breakaways on final climbs, using superior climbing form to drop companions in closing kilometers rather than launching long-range solo moves.
team_tactics: Infiltrates large breakaways early, then relies on individual climbing strength rather than team support in finale—domestiques not evident in winning effort.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with final climbs where breakaways survive; excels when able to convert pure climbing form against break companions rather than GC contenders.
vulnerability: Limited data suggests reliance on breakaway scenarios rather than direct confrontation with top GC climbers; years of attempting Grand Tour stage wins before breakthrough indicates inconsistency.
key_patterns: Operates from breakaways rather than peloton, requiring early infiltration for stage success.
Deploys climbing acceleration in final kilometers rather than sustained long-range attacks.
Multi-year conversion pattern: baby Giro winner took years to translate to Grand Tour stage victory.
form_trajectory: positive: Converted years of climbing potential into breakthrough Grand Tour stage win, suggesting maturation of racing approach
